output_text:
# Deep Research Report: Exoplanet Atmosphere Evidence

## Executive Summary

Transmission and emission spectroscopy provide complementary constraints on atmospheric composition, while clouds and retrieval-model assumptions remain important sources of uncertainty.

## Findings

- Broad wavelength coverage improves abundance constraints.
- Repeated observations help distinguish instrumental systematics from atmospheric structure.

## Risks

- Selection effects and model dependence can overstate molecular detections.

citations.title: Early Release Science of the exoplanet WASP-39b with JWST NIRISS
Sulfur dioxide in the mid-infrared transmission spectrum of WASP-39b

request.query: Survey robust evidence and uncertainty in exoplanet atmosphere characterization
